macro_summary: Markets enter July 14 navigating a confluence of geopolitical and monetary catalysts: U.S.-Iran Strait of Hormuz blockade enforcement is driving an oil supply shock with WTI near $78-87/bbl, while Fed Chair Warsh delivers congressional testimony alongside the June CPI print at 8:30 AM ET. A dense earnings corridor opens this week with TSMC reporting July 16, followed by hyperscaler prints (MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30) that will directly calibrate AI capex conviction. Tech valuations remain historically compressed relative to AI growth fundamentals, creating a setup where earnings guidance — not beats alone — will determine re-rating.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 6 qualifying ideas cleared this bar; 3 tickers appeared in 4 or more runs.
key_events: June CPI print — today July 14, 8:30 AM ET
Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony — today July 14
U.S.-Iran Strait of Hormuz blockade enforcement begins — July 14
TSMC Q2 2026 earnings — July 16 pre-market (2 days)
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings + FY2027 capex guidance — July 29 after close
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings — July 30 after close
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings — August 26 after close

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction — the strongest consensus signal in this cycle. MSFT has de-rated 30%+ from its all-time high despite AI ARR accelerating to $37B at 123% YoY growth, creating a ~56% gap to the consensus analyst target of $589-592 vs. ~$382 current. The July 29 earnings print is a binary catalyst: Azure guided 39-40% CC growth and FY2027 capex guidance will directly drive NVDA sentiment; a re-acceleration after last quarter's EPS miss ($4.06 vs. $4.24 est.) would be the re-rating trigger.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 after close — first print since EPS miss; FY2027 capex guidance expected same date
ideas.edge: Trades at 19-22x NTM EPS — cheapest forward multiple since 2023; consensus target $589-592 vs. ~$382 current (~54% implied upside); AI ARR $37B growing 123% YoY; Wedbush target $625; 97 analysts mostly Strong Buy
ideas.bull_case: Azure re-accelerates to or above 39-40% CC guidance and FY2027 capex is raised, confirming AI hyperscaler demand — stock re-rates toward $500+ as the valuation discount closes rapidly.
ideas.bear_case: Higher-for-longer rates compress multiples further and a second consecutive EPS miss on Azure deceleration triggers institutional capitulation below $360 support.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADR)
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens as the dominant HBM memory play with a post-IPO re-entry setup. SKHY listed on Nasdaq July 10 (permanent ticker live July 13-14), opened at $170 (+14% vs. $149 IPO price), then flushed -9.3% to ~$152 on profit-taking and a single brokerage note flagging near-term profit miss risk — bringing the stock back near IPO price support. With 56.4% HBM market share, IPO demand 7x oversubscribed, and CEO projecting the 'worst-ever' memory supply shortage in 2027 extending past 2030, the structural thesis remains intact at a better entry.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 SK Hynix earnings expected late July 2026; first full week of Nasdaq trading as permanent SKHY ticker beginning July 14
ideas.edge: IPO priced at $149, now ~$152 — near-offer-price support after -9% flush; 7x oversubscribed IPO demand; 56.4% HBM market share; structural memory shortage narrative unbroken; no options market yet limits leverage but also caps dealer hedging pressure
ideas.bull_case: Stock holds $149 IPO price support and Q2 earnings clear the near-term profit miss concern, reigniting institutional buying of the only pure-play HBM ADR on a US exchange.
ideas.bear_case: Q2 operating profit miss confirmed by earnings and thin ADR liquidity in early trading amplify a breakdown below $149 with no options market for hedging; new listing volatility risk is elevated.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 4 of 6 screens with majority high conviction. NVDA trades at ~21.7x forward earnings — roughly in line with the S&P 500 — despite 85% YoY revenue growth, 97% GPU market share, and HBM supply sold out through most of 2027. Goldman Sachs notes the forward P/E is near a multi-decade low vs. its 5-year average of 72x. The TSMC Q2 print on July 16 is a 48-hour read-through catalyst, with hyperscaler capex guidance (MSFT July 29) as the primary re-rating trigger ahead of NVDA's own August 26 earnings.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC Q2 earnings July 16 (read-through in 2 days); MSFT FY2027 capex guidance July 29; NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026 after close
ideas.edge: Forward P/E ~21.7x vs. 5-yr avg 72x (Goldman Sachs); stock ~35-50% below analyst consensus targets (Rosenblatt PT $325 vs. ~$200 current = ~60% upside); $80B buyback authorized; 58 Buy ratings; semis sector Q2 EPS growth expected +131%
ideas.bull_case: TSMC confirms robust CoWoS and HBM demand July 16, MSFT raises capex guidance July 29, and NVDA's August 26 print delivers another 85%+ revenue growth quarter — closes the gap to $300+ targets rapidly.
ideas.bear_case: Iran blockade spooks risk assets, Fed holds hawkish tone, and any softness in hyperscaler capex commentary triggers a second leg down from $200 with no fundamental change in AI spend timing.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 4 of 6 overnight screens, though with mixed conviction (2 high, 2 medium) reflecting a stretched valuation debate. AAPL reports July 30 with three stacked catalysts: Q3 FY2026 earnings (8 consecutive EPS beats, iPhone +22% last quarter, Services at $31B/qtr), a confirmed CEO transition to John Ternus effective September 1, and ~92% probability of a foldable iPhone launch pre-2027. The asymmetric options setup — not outright stock — is the consensus preferred structure given AAPL trades at 37-38x earnings with consensus target already near current price (~$315-317).
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; TSMC July 16 confirms 2nm iPhone chip ramp; CEO transition to John Ternus effective September 1
ideas.edge: 8 consecutive EPS beats; Services segment $31B/qtr at 32% margins; $100B buyback ongoing; foldable iPhone optionality not yet priced; bull case analyst target $350 (~10% upside) vs. bear case $299 (-6%) — binary skew into July 30 favors options over stock
ideas.bull_case: Q3 beats on iPhone units and Services margin expansion, CEO transition is executed cleanly, and foldable iPhone confirmation adds a new product cycle premium — stock breaks to $350.
ideas.bear_case: 38x valuation leaves no margin for error; CEO uncertainty and macro softness pressure Services growth guidance, while TSMC wafer price hikes of 5-10% squeeze hardware margins — stock revisits $280-290.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 3 of 6 screens with all 3 registering high conviction — the highest per-appearance conviction rate in this digest. TSMC reports Q2 2026 earnings in 2 days (July 16 pre-market) with June revenue already reported at +68% YoY, exceeding the high end of its own $40.2B guidance. Prediction markets assign a 95% beat probability, Citi placed it on a 30-day upside catalyst watch, and the stock remains a read-through bellwether for NVDA, AAPL, and AMD demand.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report July 16, 2026 pre-market — 2 days away; Citi 30-day upside catalyst watch active
ideas.edge: Avg analyst target ~$520 vs. ~$432-434 current (~20% implied upside); Barclays PT $625 (+38% upside); 4 consecutive quarters of avg 8.34% EPS beat; June revenue already exceeded high-end guidance at +68% YoY; stock underperformed SOX index by ~32% YTD — meaningful catch-up potential
ideas.bull_case: Q2 beat confirmed with N2 ramp guidance raise and CoWoS capacity expansion commentary — catalyzes re-rating across the entire AI semiconductor supply chain toward the $520-625 target range.
ideas.bear_case: Beat is fully priced in at 95% prediction-market probability; wafer price hike announcements (5-10% to AAPL, NVDA, AMD) create downstream margin concern and sell-the-news reaction compresses the stock despite the beat.

ideas.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 6 screens as the consensus geopolitical/inflation hedge. U.S.-Iran Strait of Hormuz blockade enforcement begins today (July 14) alongside Trump's newly announced 20% 'protection tax' on Hormuz cargo, creating an immediate oil supply shock. XLE rose 3.2% on July 13 while the Nasdaq fell 1.6% — a clear sector rotation signal — and energy remains the only S&P sector clearly bid in Monday's risk-off tape with WTI at $78-87/bbl and rising.
ideas.catalyst: U.S.-Iran Strait of Hormuz blockade enforcement active July 14; June CPI print today at 8:30 AM ET may confirm sustained energy inflation; ongoing Trump 'protection tax' on Hormuz cargo announced July 13
ideas.edge: XLE +3.2% vs. XLK -2.1% on July 13 — clear rotation; current ~$55 vs. 52-week high $63.46 (~15% to prior highs); WTI up sharply; 0.08% expense ratio; options-eligible for defined-risk structures
ideas.bull_case: Sustained Hormuz disruption keeps WTI above $85, CPI print confirms energy-driven inflation stickiness, and sector rotation from tech into energy accelerates — XLE recovers toward $63 highs.
ideas.bear_case: Geopolitical catalyst de-escalates quickly (diplomatic resolution or OPEC+ response), oil reverses, and the CPI print shows moderation — XLE gives back the Monday move rapidly as a pure event-driven spike.
